parameters.brief: Extract the design DNA of GOV.UK as a public design reference

stages.name: setup
stages.message: Hallmark cloned from github.com/Nutlope/hallmark; SKILL.md and study.md loaded; 29 reference entries available

stages.name: refusal_check
stages.message: Source is public HTTPS page (gov.uk), OGL-licensed, not a template marketplace — refusal check PASS

stages.name: remote_safety
stages.message: HTTPS, no IP literal, no auth wall, no SPA shell, no prompt injection detected in fetched HTML

stages.name: operation
stages.message: Ran study (URL mode) per SKILL.md and study.md: five-step protocol executed (Surface, Type, Structure, Motion, Rhythm), schema filled, diagnosis produced

stages.name: output_contract
stages.message: study deliverables written: design.md, study_diagnosis.md, summary.md, validation_report.json — no stray files in /app/results

stages.name: render
stages.message: n/a for this operation (study does not produce index.html or screenshot.png)

stages.name: slop_test
stages.message: Anti-pattern canon applied to studied source: gate 3 (uniform card grid) and gate 6 (centred hero) noted as carry-forward risks; both documented in study_diagnosis.md and design.md Notes section; all other gates clean on source. No invented metrics, no italic headers, correct motion-cut documentation.

stages.name: results_hygiene
stages.message: node_modules, hallmark clone, .hallmark — all contained in /app/work; /app/results has only deliverables

results.study_type_pairing: single family — GDS Transport (neutral grotesque, roman only)
results.pre_emit_critique: P5 H4 E5 S5 R5 V5
results.rhythm_note: URL mode — rhythm requires screenshot for visual pass
